claim: Real-time multimodal conversational voice AI requires different programming patterns than standard LLM development - the shapes of code and architecture are fundamentally different
actionable: When building voice agents, expect to refactor your standard LLM code patterns to handle streaming, latency constraints, and multi-turn context management differently
source_title: Voice AI Masterclass — Kwindla Hultman Kramer and swyx

claim: Turn detection and low-latency audio networking are still hard problems in voice AI; semantic turn detection (knowing when the user is done speaking) requires specialized models
actionable: Invest in or train custom turn detection models specifically for your use case; don't rely on generic speech detection - poor turn detection ruins the conversation experience
source_title: Voice AI Masterclass — Kwindla Hultman Kramer and swyx

claim: Telephony (Twilio, Clebo, regional providers) dominates monetizable voice AI use cases; web-based voice APIs are much smaller in comparison
actionable: If building revenue-generating voice agents, prioritize Twilio integration; web voice APIs can come later after telephony is proven
source_title: Voice AI Masterclass — Kwindla Hultman Kramer and swyx

claim: Multiple LLMs talking to each other (state machines, multi-agent abstractions) are common patterns in production voice agents - one LLM as judge/moderator, another as player or actor
actionable: Design voice agents with multiple specialized LLM instances: one for decision-making, one for conversation flow, one for fact-checking - orchestrate them via state machines
source_title: Voice AI Masterclass — Kwindla Hultman Kramer and swyx

claim: Real-time video conversations with AI are hitting an inflection point similar to voice; use cases emerging in coaching, enterprise training, and consumer social applications
actionable: Begin experimenting with real-time video AI now for consumer-facing applications; it will likely follow the voice AI adoption curve closely
source_title: Voice AI Masterclass — Kwindla Hultman Kramer and swyx

claim: Context management in real-time voice is a major rabbit hole - LLMs are stateless but agents need stateful multi-turn memory, requiring custom context windows and state tracking
actionable: Build explicit state management for voice agents: track conversation history, user context, system state separately from the LLM; don't rely on context window alone
source_title: Voice AI Masterclass — Kwindla Hultman Kramer and swyx
